description: Characters whose palette entries must be unambiguously warm (R >= G and R >= B, within soft_tolerance). Add character prefix codes to warm_prefixes to extend coverage beyond Grandma Miri (CHAR-M). Set soft_tolerance.B > 0 (e.g. 10) to allow warm-adjacent dusty lavenders/grays where B may slightly exceed R without flagging. Default: strict (tolerance 0 for both channels). Use --strict CLI flag to force strict mode regardless of config.

real_world_interior.note: Lamp-lit warm interior with cyan monitor accent. SF01 archetype.

real_world_night_storm.note: Night/storm scene — cold dominant, warm building-window accents only. SF02 archetype.

glitch_world.note: Warm tones are PROHIBITED in Glitch World. Near-zero warm/cool ratio is CORRECT. Warm presence (not absence) would be a FAIL. SF03/SF04 archetype.

_world_presets_note: Per-world warm/cool thresholds for LTG_TOOL_render_qa_v001._check_warm_cool(). Currently informational — Kai Nakamura to integrate --world-type parameter into render_qa tool. C35 art director decision: Alex Chen warm_cool_decision_c35.md.
